_doc: Harbor & Oak Candle Co. — the entire fictional narrative for the Command Center demo pack. ZERO real client data. All names/companies are invented. Consumed by scripts/demo/seed-demo.ts. Per-dept CURRENT/PREVIOUS window counts are engineered against the REAL grading engine (src/lib/grading.ts) to yield: sales=A, research=C (worst-trending), the other 7=B, company=B.

memories.content: Goal: Grow repeat-purchase rate to 35% via a fall scent-of-the-month email arc.
Constraint: Every asset must use the Harbor & Oak deep-green + warm-gold palette.
Lesson: Story-led candle-care emails outperform discount blasts 3x on open rate.
Goal: Land 12 boutique wholesale accounts this quarter.
Lesson: Sample boxes shipped within 24h of inquiry convert at 2.5x.
Goal: Hold CSAT above 90% through the Q4 holiday rush.
Context: Top tickets are melted-in-transit candles and scent-swap requests.
Goal: Sub-2s product-page load on the shop storefront.
Lesson: Lazy-loading the scent-gallery images cut LCP by 40%.
Goal: One cohesive 'burn night' content pillar per week.
Lesson: Pour-process reels outperform flat-lay photos 4x on saves.
Goal: 12 short-form pours + care clips per month across Reels and Shorts.
Constraint: Every clip captioned and cut for 9:16, 1:1, and 16:9.
Goal: Ship a monthly scent-trend brief two weeks ahead of buying decisions.
Lesson: Momentum slipped this cycle — the trend brief shipped late and thin (worst-trending).
Goal: Reconcile Stripe payouts weekly and keep collection above 95%.
Context: 70% of revenue is DTC one-time orders, 30% wholesale net-30.
Constraint: Every candle label must carry the CPSC-compliant burn-safety warning.
Lesson: Pre-approved wholesale terms cut contract review time nearly in half.

messages.content: Morning, team. Q4 candle season is spinning up. Priorities today: Marketing's scent-of-the-month arc, Sales' wholesale sample boxes, and Research owes us the trend brief.
Scent-of-the-month arc is drafted — three emails, all on the deep-green and gold palette. Sending to QC now.
Two boutique accounts verbally in. Sample boxes ship today so we stay inside the 24-hour window.
Trend brief is behind. I'll have the short version to the board by end of day — flagging it so it doesn't slip again.
Appreciated, Iman. Get me the short version; we can deepen it next cycle. Nice work everyone.

campaigns.name: Autumn Ember Launch
campaigns.description: Q4 fall/holiday scent line — cross-department push across marketing, social, video, and sales.

campaigns.name: Wholesale Sample Box Program
campaigns.description: Sample-box outreach to independent boutiques, supported by billing terms and legal wholesale agreements.

## Synthesis

Harbor & Oak Candle Co. is a small e-commerce home-fragrance brand run by founder Maya Okafor with a handful of part-time helpers. Identity and brand voice are captured; the exact brand COLOR was deliberately left for the owner to choose live on the color card. Operations are partially mapped (fulfillment out of a small Savannah studio, busiest in Q4). Department reconciliation is nearly complete — three departments (podcast, paid advertisement, presentations) are still to be decided live on the board.

transcript.q: What is the name of your business?
transcript.provenance: confirmed-from-context: owner stated the legal and trading name directly.

transcript.q: In one or two sentences, what does the business do and what industry is it in?
transcript.a: We are an e-commerce home-fragrance brand. We hand-pour small-batch soy candles and sell them direct-to-consumer online, with a growing wholesale side to independent boutiques.
transcript.provenance: confirmed-from-context: owner described product, channel, and industry.

transcript.q: Who owns or runs the business day to day?
transcript.a: I do — Maya Okafor, the founder. I make the final call on brand, product, and money.
transcript.provenance: confirmed-from-context: owner self-identified as founder and decision-maker.

transcript.q: How big is the team right now?
transcript.a: Small. It's me full-time plus four part-time helpers who handle pouring, packing, and social when things get busy.
transcript.provenance: confirmed-from-context: owner gave headcount and how roles split.

transcript.q: What name or short-hand do customers actually use for the brand?
transcript.a: Most people just say 'Harbor & Oak', and regulars shorten it to 'H&O'.
transcript.provenance: confirmed-from-context: owner confirmed the customer-facing brand name and nickname.

transcript.q: Do you have a tagline or one line that captures the brand?
transcript.a: Yes — 'Light that feels like home.' It's on the packaging and the site header.
transcript.provenance: confirmed-from-context: owner supplied the working tagline verbatim.

transcript.q: How would you describe the brand's voice or tone?
transcript.a: Warm, grounded, and unhurried. Like a friend who knows candles but never lectures you about them.
transcript.provenance: confirmed-from-context: owner described tone in her own words.

transcript.q: How do orders get fulfilled once someone buys?
transcript.a: Everything ships from our little studio in Savannah. Checkout runs through Stripe, and we pack and hand off to USPS the same or next day.
transcript.provenance: confirmed-from-context: owner walked through the fulfillment path and payment tool.

transcript.a: Q4, hands down. September through December is the holiday and gifting rush — it's most of our year.
transcript.provenance: confirmed-from-context: owner named the peak months and why.
